# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14

# Update system packages
RUN apt-get update -y

# Install build-essential for compiling source code, libpq-dev for PostgreSQL support, and wget for downloading dependencies
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential libpq-dev wget

# Create app directory
RUN mkdir -p /app

# Move into the app directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to the app directory
COPY package*.json ./

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm install

# Expose application port (change if necessary)
EXPOSE 3000

# Start the application when container starts
CMD ["npm", "start"]
